title: AN ACT TO FURTHER AMEND SECTION TWO OF REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED ELEVEN   HUNDRED AND FIFTY-ONE, AS AMENDED BY REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED THIRTY-EIGHT HUNDRED AND SEVEN, BY ADJUSTING THE SALARIES OF THE OFFICIALS OF THE Land REGISTRATION COMMISSION.

chunks.text:
H. No. 8599 / 61 OG No. 3, 253 (January 18, 1965)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 4040, June 18, 1964 ]

AN ACT TO FURTHER AMEND SECTION TWO OF REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED ELEVEN HUNDRED AND FIFTY-ONE, AS AMENDED BY REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED THIRTY-EIGHT HUNDRED AND SEVEN, BY ADJUSTING THE SALARIES OF THE OFFICIALS OF THE Land REGISTRATION COMMISSION.

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. Section two of Republic Act Numbered Eleven hundred and fifty-one, as amended by Republic Act Numbered Thirty-eight hundred and seven, is hereby further amended to read as follows:

"SEC. 2.

Chief and Assistant Chief and other ofÂficials of the Commission.

&mdashThe Land Registration Commission shall have a chief and an assistant chief, to be known, respectively, as the Commissioner and the Assistant Commissioner of Land Registration who shall be appointed by the President with the consent of the Commission on Appointments. The Commissioner shall be a duly qualified member of the Philippines Bar with at least five years of practice in the legal profession, and shall be entitled to the same compensation, emoluments and privileges as those of a Judge of the Court of First Instance. The Assistant Commissioner who shall possess the same qualifications as those required of the Commissioner, shall receive compensation at the rate of twelve thousand five hundred sixteen pesos

per annum.

SEC. 2. Provisions of any law to the contrary notwithstanding, the incumbent officials mentioned in Section two of Republic Act Numbered Eleven hundred and fifty-one, as hereby amended, shall continue to occupy their respective positions and receive the salary rates provided for in this Act:

Provided,

That no attorney already holding office as such in the Law Division of the Land Registration Commission and affected by the preceding section upon the passage of this Act shall, by reason thereof, be removed from office or demoted to a lower category or scale of salary except for cause and upon compliance with the due processes provided by law.

SEC. 3. The salary differentials resulting from the adjustment in salaries provided in this Act shall be paid out the savings in the appropriation for the Land Registration Commission during the first fiscal year. In subsequent years the same shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved, June 18, 1964.
